claim: Explorer and cross-checker evidence is sufficient to support PRD readiness: boundary audit implementation is complete, scope validation enforces evidence discipline, 102 real-world dispatch runs show clean boundaries, and no assumptions are presented as facts.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() and diffBoundarySnapshots() implement SHA256-based file tracking and git integration.
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation confirmed by both explorer and cross-checker.

evidence.summary: 102 dispatch artifact directories matching dispatch-*.json pattern, spanning timestamps from 2026-06-01T03:57:29Z to 2026-06-01T06:02:39Z.
evidence.why: Production artifact evidence independently verified by both agents, proving extensive real-world stress-testing.

evidence.summary: Scope validation enforces project, external, user, or unknown scope declaration on all evidence records.
evidence.why: Evidence discipline enforcement confirmed by both explorer and cross-checker.

evidence.summary: Test suite proves boundary audit detects both tracked and untracked project mutations and blocks PRD readiness on violations.
evidence.why: Test evidence independently confirmed by both agents showing mutation detection works.

risks: If boundary audit had false negatives or cross-checker missed conflicts, PRD readiness could be granted on incomplete evidence.

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker completed with high confidence, pass verdicts, and no conflicts. All evidence has proper scope (project) with concrete paths. No assumptions presented as facts. No missing data. Production artifacts (102 dispatch runs) prove real-world stress-testing with clean boundaries. Scope validation enforces evidence discipline. PRD readiness criteria are satisfied.
